hypothesis: If an EfficientNet-B0 model pretrained on a data-rich, additive-free SEM dataset is fine-tuned on the small additive-induced SEM dataset using targeted oversampling to balance classes, then it will achieve higher class-wise accuracy and F1 scores for NCM composition and aging-state classification than models trained from scratch, models fine-tuned with weighted loss without oversampling, and alternative architectures (ResNet-18, MobileNet-v2, DenseNet-201), because domain-specific transfer learning captures morphological features that improve generalization in the data-poor target domain.
